title: Phenol-based RNA extraction from polycarbonate filters
author: Robbie M. Martin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bivuke6w
materials: Carefully decant ethanol supernatant without disturbing RNA pellet.

steps.title: Add 700 µL of Solution A...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL of Solution A (lysing buffer) to an MP Biomedical Lysing Matrix E tube (or equivalent bead beating tube). Solution A consists of 0.5% SDS, 20 mM sodium acetate, and 10 mM EDTA in RNase-free molecular biology grade water.

steps.title: Pre-chill tube containing Solution A on...
steps.instruction: Pre-chill tube containing Solution A on ice to prevent tube from overheating during bead beating.

steps.title: Add sodium acetate to aqueous supernatant...
steps.instruction: Add sodium acetate to aqueous supernatant to a concentration of 0.3 M and mix.

steps.title: Add 2-2
steps.instruction: Add 2-2.5x volume of ice-cold 100% ethanol and mix well.

steps.title: Place in -80 °C freezer for...
steps.instruction: Place in -80 °C freezer for at least 1 h, or preferably overnight.

steps.title: Unfold polycarbonate filter (either frozen, or...
steps.instruction: Unfold polycarbonate filter (either frozen, or thawed but maintained on ice) and place into pre-chilled bead-beating tube.

steps.title: Agitate tube on maximum speed for...
steps.instruction: Agitate tube on maximum speed for 40 s in Biospec Products Mini-beadbeater (or equivalent), then place on ice.

steps.title: Add 500 µL basic phenol with 8-hydroxyquinoline (pH 7
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL basic phenol with 8-hydroxyquinoline (pH 7.8-8.2) plus 100 µL chloroform to tube.

steps.title: Agitate tube on maximum speed for...
steps.instruction: Agitate tube on maximum speed for 40 s, then place briefly on ice to chill.

steps.title: Centrifuge tube at maximum speed in...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tube at maximum speed in benchtop centrifuge for 5 min.

steps.title: Transfer as much of aqueous supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer as much of aqueous supernatant as possible from bead beating tube to new 2 mL centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Re-extract aqueous supernatant twice with 500...
steps.instruction: Re-extract aqueous supernatant twice with 500 µL of acid phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (125:24:1; pH 4.8).

steps.title: Re-extract aqueous supernatant once with 500...
steps.instruction: Re-extract aqueous supernatant once with 500 µL basic phenol with 8-hydroxyquinoline plus 100 µL chloroform.

steps.title: Re-extract twice with 500 µL pure...
steps.instruction: Re-extract twice with 500 µL pure chloroform to remove last traces of residual phenol.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed at 0...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed at 0 °C for at least 30 min in refrigerated benchtop centrifuge. If you expect low yields, or wish to increase recovery, spin for 1 h at maximum.

steps.title: Wash pellet by adding 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash pellet by adding 1 mL of ice-cold 70% ethanol and gently pipetting to dislodge RNA pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed at 0...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed at 0 °C for at least 15 min in a refrigerated benchtop centrifuge.

steps.title: Gently and carefully aspirate as much...
steps.instruction: Gently and carefully aspirate as much of the 70% ethanol from the tube as possible taking caution not to accidently aspirate the RNA pellet.

steps.title: Place the open tube in a...
steps.instruction: Place the open tube in a 37 °C heating block for 3-5 min to evaporate all ethanol. Do not dry pellet to completion, as this can make RNA difficult to redissolve in water.

steps.title: Dissolve the RNA pellet in an...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the RNA pellet in an amount of RNase-free water appropriate for your expected yield and needed final concentration, typically about 50-100 µL.
